title: MongoDB Q4 FY2026 Earnings Recap
text: MongoDB reported strong fourth quarter fiscal 2026 results with revenue of $695.1 million, up 27% year-over-year, and full year fiscal 2026 revenue of $2.46 billion, up 23% year-over-year. Atlas growth reached 29% in Q4, representing continued momentum in the company's strategic cloud offering. Non-Atlas business accelerated to 20% growth, indicating diversification of revenue streams. The company added 2,700 customers in the quarter, achieving over 65,200 total customers with 60% new customer growth in fiscal 2026. Despite strong quarterly performance, MongoDB's stock suffered a massive 22.24% collapse following the earnings announcement. The selloff reflected concerns about Q1 FY2027 guidance, which signaled potential growth moderation with revenue growth expected to slow from fiscal 2026 levels. Management guided to $2.86-$2.90 billion in FY2027 revenue, representing 16-18% growth compared to 23% growth in FY2026. The guidance disappointed investors accustomed to the company's historical growth rates, resulting in significant valuation repricing.
